qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: JEXL expression evaluation with unrestricted permissions enables remote code execution via nocode YAML configuration
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes unsafe deserialization in RMI instrumentation (missing serialization filters on incoming RMI/JMX data). This finding describes a different vulnerability: JEXL expression evaluation with JexlPermissions.UNRESTRICTED in the nocode instrumentation module. Different vulnerable module (nocode/JexlEvaluator vs RMI instrumentation), different root cause (expression language injection via config file vs deserialization without filters), different attack vector (local config file manipulation vs network RMI port access).

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code/root cause. The advisory describes unsafe deserialization in the RMI instrumentation module of splunk-otel-javaagent, where incoming data on JMX/RMI ports is deserialized without serialization filters. Finding 'jexl_unrestricted_rce' targets the same repository but a different module (nocode JexlEvaluator) with a different root cause (JEXL expression evaluation with UNRESTRICTED permissions vs missing deserialization filters).
evidence: Advisory root cause: RMI instrumentation registers custom endpoint that deserializes incoming data without serialization filters (CWE-502).
Finding jexl_unrestricted_rce root cause: JexlEvaluator uses JexlPermissions.UNRESTRICTED in the nocode module — expression language injection, not deserialization.
Advisory reference (GHSA-xw7x-h9fj-p2c7) specifically describes RMI instrumentation; the finding addresses a different instrumentation module (nocode).
